items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 residential development approved on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four new homes permitted as part of the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development along Altino Boulevard (lots 19-23), ranging from $487K to $573K. This represents a significant residential infill project adding ~5 units to the neighborhood.

items.headline: Sherly Place three-lot residential project moves forward
items.summary: Three new homes approved for Sherly Place (lots 2-4), ranging from $496K to $570K. This smaller but complete residential infill project adds three units to the area.

items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: Mountain View is evaluating its major infrastructure and development spending plans for the next fiscal year. This study session will detail what projects the city prioritizes for funding, which could affect everything from transportation to parks to housing development.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance amending the Residential Tenant Protections Program, which governs renter rights and landlord regulations in Sunnyvale. The specific changes were not detailed in the agenda, but this could affect housing costs, eviction rules, or other tenant-landlord policies for local renters.

items.headline: City releases Children and Youth Services Master Plan status update
items.summary: The City Council received a status report on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, which outlines the city's strategy for youth programs and services. This plan guides how San José allocates resources to youth empowerment, summer safety initiatives, and intervention services.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan moves forward
items.summary: The City Council reviewed progress on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, which addresses improvements to San José's library system and user experience. This plan will shape future renovations and service upgrades at local branches.

items.headline: Downtown building seeks historic status upgrade and 5,000 sq ft bonus
items.summary: A property at 405 Kipling Street is requesting elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for historic and seismic rehabilitation. This could enable a notable expansion of the downtown building if approved.
